sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 4.57 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.4)

statement.latex: Let a group$G$be the product of two of its abelian minimax subgroups$A$and~$B$. Prove or refute the following statements:$A_0B_0 1$, where$A_0= _{x G}A^x$and similarly for$B_0$; the derived subgroup of$G$is a minimax subgroup. { N.\,F.\,Sesekin} Both parts were proved (D.\,I.\,Zaitsev, Algebra and Logic, { 19} (1980), 94--106).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
